recognized_artifact: No confident recognition. The abstract shape (expand one state into multiple candidate paths; introduce a differentiating input; re-sort candidate fit; loop while separation is weak; close when one candidate is strongest, or abandon when continuation is not justified) resembles a generic iterative candidate-discrimination / hypothesis-refinement loop (family that includes e.g. differential-diagnosis-style procedures or elimination-by-evidence loops). No domain terminology is present in the packet, so no specific named artifact is identified.

exit_conditions.ambiguity: Packet does not state whether this exit represents success, abandonment, or an inconclusive outcome

loops.ambiguity: Whether reopening via N5 revisits all N2 nodes or a subset is not stated; no maximum iteration count is given

Three-way decision at N4: continue loop (N5), select and close (N6), or terminate directly (END)

branches.ambiguity: Relative priority or mutual exclusivity of the three conditions is inferred from edge labels but not formally stated

branches.value: Fan-out at N5 back into the three N2 paths
branches.ambiguity: Same parallel-vs-selective ambiguity as N1 fan-out

structural_layer.control_flow_shape: Single entry; fan-out into three parallel-or-alternative branches; convergence into a differentiating-input step; evaluation/re-sorting step with a three-way conditional; a re-entrant loop through a reopening node back into the branch layer; two distinct termination routes (selected closure via N6, direct exit from N4).

transitions: evaluate -> reopen (threshold not met) -> branch traversal

candidate_missing_primitives: initial candidate-generation criteria (how the three paths are defined)
any per-branch processing semantics for N2A/N2B/N2C (no node notes exist for these nodes)

representation_layer.natural_language_summary: The procedure starts from a single state, expands it into three candidate paths (N2A, N2B, N2C), and converges them into a step that adds a differentiating input (N3). A re-sorting step (N4) then re-evaluates how well each path fits given the new input. From N4, three outcomes are possible: if one path is clearly strongest, a closing step (N6) selects it and the procedure ends; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends directly; if the threshold for separation is not met, a reopening step (N5) sends the process back through the candidate paths to gather another differentiating input and re-sort again. This loop repeats until one of the two termination conditions is reached.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: Expand -> branch (3 candidates) -> add discriminating input -> re-rank -> {select & close | terminate unjustified | reopen & iterate}.

plausible_interpretations: Parallel traversal: all candidate paths are processed each iteration
Selective traversal: one candidate path is followed per iteration
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Branch traversal mode left as UNSPECIFIED in the AST; both readings preserved rather than collapsed

ambiguities.location: N4 -> END [continuation not justified] vs N4 -> N6 [one path selected]
ambiguities.description: Two distinct terminating outcomes exist but the packet does not define the semantic difference (e.g., success vs. abandonment).
plausible_interpretations: Direct END is an abandonment/no-result exit while N6 is a successful selection
Direct END is an alternative closure where selection is unnecessary
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Both exits recorded as termination conditions without assigning success/failure semantics

plausible_interpretations: They describe the same condition in different words
'threshold' is a formalization of 'separation' evaluated at N4, with N5 applying an additional reopening judgment
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Treated as one condition with the equivalence flagged as unconfirmed

recognition_analysis.recognition_basis: Abstract control-flow shape only: expand-candidates / add-discriminating-input / re-rank / loop-until-separation pattern resembles a generic iterative hypothesis-discrimination or candidate-elimination loop. No domain terms, labels, or content in the packet identify a specific artifact.
recognition_analysis.prior_knowledge_leakage_risk: Low-to-moderate. The resemblance noted in recognized_artifact could bias interpretation toward diagnosis-style semantics; extraction was restricted to packet vocabulary (paths, differentiating input, re-sorting, separation) and no domain terminology was imported into any layer.

notes: Edge order in the packet was shuffled; the reconstruction above is order-independent and derived solely from edge connectivity plus node notes. All three N4 outcomes are explicitly labeled in the packet, which is the strongest structural signal. Nodes N2A/N2B/N2C are structurally symmetric (identical in-edges from N1 and N5 and identical out-edges to N3), so no differentiation among them was assumed. No iteration bound, threshold value, or definition of 'continuation not justified' exists in the packet; these were recorded as missing rather than inferred.
